---
title: "Bora Kang"
subtitle: "Ph.D. in English"
image: images/BAB58528-B764-40DC-8037-FC4E760A8CB8.jpg

about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 10em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:bkang21@binghamton.edu"
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=_4tWMuwAAAAJ&hl=en"
---

## About

I am a scholar of Anglophone literature specializing in modern and contemporary British literature, film and media, visual studies, and cultural studies. I received my Ph.D. in English from Binghamton University. My research examines how literary and cultural forms shape ideas of Englishness, national belonging, and collective memory through affective experience.

## Research & Teaching Interests

- Modern and Contemporary Anglophone Literature
- British Literature and Cultural Identity
- Emotion and Affect Theory
- Narrative and Seriality in Screen Media
- Visuality and Perception
- Heritage Media and Cultural Memory
